summary: Round-2 re-review of Test_DiffZeroFilledCells. All three round-1 findings are resolved or acceptably handled. The test remains a genuinely behavioral closing-debt test (litmus-passing: neutralizing diff()'s delta computation breaks the mixed-row 50 assertion). Minor #2 is fixed: the Compare assertion now calls Compare(... skey=0, desc=true, ukey=0 ...) and asserts the diff rows are reordered to d4,b2,a1 — diff()'s natural output order is a1,b2,d4, so the DESC reorder genuinely exercises sort() and would fail if sort were a no-op; it also re-asserts the a1 reads=0 / hits=50 deltas survive the wrapper. Minor #3 is fixed: the empty-cell sub-case uses assert.ErrorContains(t, err, "convert '' to int failed"), which matches parsePairInt's actual wrap (fmt.Errorf("convert '%s' to int failed: %w", ...) at postgres.go:480/484), binding the error to the empty cell. The major verify-mask issue is handled as a documented deviation: the task constraint fixes the new test name to match -run Diff, an in-code NOTE directs readers to -run '[Dd]iff' for full regression coverage, and verification runs both masks. Empirically confirmed: -run Diff selects only Test_DiffZeroFilledCells (PASS); -run '[Dd]iff' selects Test_diff, Test_DiffZeroFilledCells, Test_diff_pg18_wal_stats_age, Test_diffPair (all PASS). The regression-safety intent is preserved by the dual-mask verification, so the residual case-sensitivity gap is acknowledged and mitigated rather than left silent. No remaining critical or major findings.
